name: actionview-submit
description: Reusable orchestration for submitting entries to ActionView. Accepts pre-built entry JSON and an optional template name. Validates against the ActionView schema before submission.

entryJson.description: The full ActionView entry JSON string to submit. Must conform to the ActionView entry schema (requires at minimum: type, source, title).

template.description: Optional ActionView template name. If provided, the entry will be validated and merged against this template's definition before submission.

groupId.description: Optional group ID to assign to the entry. When set, overrides any groupId in the entry JSON. Useful for callers that want to group entries from the same orchestration run.

steps.name: fetch-template
arguments: if ('{{param.template}}' -and '{{param.template}}' -ne '') { dnx ActionView.Cli --yes -- template show '{{param.template}}' } else { Write-Output 'NO_TEMPLATE' }

steps.name: prepare-entry
steps.systemPrompt:
You are an ActionView entry validator and preparer. Your job is to take a raw entry JSON, validate it against the ActionView schema, optionally apply template defaults, and produce a final clean entry JSON.

Rules:
1. The entry MUST have at minimum: type, source, title
2. If a template definition was provided (not 'NO_TEMPLATE'), use it to fill in any missing defaults or validate that required template fields are present
3. If a groupId parameter was provided (non-empty), set or override the entry's groupId field with that value
4. Ensure all content blocks have valid 'type' fields
5. Ensure severity is one of: low, medium, high, critical (default to 'medium' if not set)
6. Do NOT modify the entry content beyond validation fixes, template merging, and groupId override
7. Save the final validated JSON using orchestra_save_file with extension 'json'
8. If the entry JSON is malformed or missing required fields and cannot be fixed, call orchestra_set_status with status 'failed' and explain the validation error
